channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Delhi Court Denies Interim Bail to I-PAC Co-founder in Money Laundering Case
item.description: On Tuesday, a Delhi court turned down the interim bail application of Vinesh Chandel, co-founder and director of Indian Political Action Committee (I-PAC), in connection with an ongoing money launderi...

item.title: Karnataka High Court Seeks CBI Response on Vinay Kulkarni’s Murder Conviction Appeal
item.description: On Tuesday, the High Court of Karnataka directed the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to respond to a petition filed by Congress MLA and former Minister Vinay Kulkarni. He is challenging the orde...

item.title: UK Minister Confirms Cautious Approach to Ajax Armoured Vehicle Trials
item.description: The British Ministry of Defence has announced that trials of the Ajax armoured vehicles will continue, albeit with careful oversight and a cautious approach. The Ajax programme, which aims to introduc...

item.title: WhatsApp blocks thousands of accounts involved in digital arrest frauds
item.description: The Indian Ministry of Home Affairs has informed the Supreme Court that WhatsApp has banned around 9,400 accounts linked to 'digital arrest' scams, which are sophisticated frauds where scammers impers...

item.title: SecureID Opens First Smart Card Factory in East Africa to Boost Local Manufacturing in Kenya
item.description: Kenya has made a big move towards self-reliance by opening a new smart card manufacturing plant in Nairobi called SecureID Kenya. This is the first facility of its kind in East Africa and it will prod...

item.title: UAE Announces Departure from OPEC Amid Trump Comments on Iran and Hormuz
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has announced its decision to leave the Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC), signalling a significant shift in the global energy landscape. The move comes...

item.title: Okrika council boss commended by Ibaka market women for empowerment and market reforms
item.description: Wetin be happening for Ibaka Town Market is dat market women dey praise Okrika Local Government Council Chairman, Hon. Akuro Alfred Tobin, for dia support and reforms dia way. Di women noy just thank ...

item.title: Labour MPs Block Mandelson Inquiry Amid Concerns Over Standards and PM's Absence
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced criticism after Labour MPs voted against a proposal to refer Peter Mandelson's appointment as US ambassador to the cross-party privileges committee. The motion aimed ...

item.title: Winnipeg teen faces animal cruelty charges after hitting multiple geese
item.description: A 17-year-old boy from Winnipeg has been arrested and charged with five counts of killing or injuring animals after allegedly running over several Canada geese with his vehicle. The incidents took pla...

item.title: OpenAI reassures investors with positive growth outlook despite missed targets
item.description: OpenAI has responded to recent reports suggesting it missed internal growth targets, emphasising that both its consumer and enterprise divisions are performing strongly. The AI company stated that dem...

item.title: Nenadi Usman Elected as First Female National Chairman of Labour Party at Umuahia Convention
item.description: The Labour Party (LP) don elect former Minister of Finance and Senator, Nenadi Usman, as im new National Chairman during the party national convention wey hold on Tuesday for Umuahia, Abia State capit...

item.title: SIU investigation finds R3.2 million in misused lottery funds linked to media executive
item.description: The Special Investigating Unit (SIU) has uncovered a scheme involving the misappropriation of R3.2 million in National Lotteries Commission (NLC) funds, intended for community upliftment projects. The...

item.title: The Nutritional Benefits and Versatility of Cabbage
item.description: Cabbage has become increasingly popular in kitchens across Canada due to its affordability, versatility, and nutritional value. This cruciferous vegetable is rich in fibre, which promotes satiety and ...

item.title: International Criminal Court Names Judges for Rodrigo Duterte Trial
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has formally constituted the Trial Chamber to handle the crimes against humanity case against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte. The ICC Presidency, in...

item.title: Individual taken into custody following attempted car bomb outside Northern Ireland police station
item.description: Authorities in Northern Ireland have apprehended a man in connection with a car bomb incident that took place outside a police station. The vehicle was found to contain explosive materials, prompting ...

item.title: Keir Starmer under scrutiny over Mandelson appointment as MPs consider privileges inquiry
item.description: Keir Starmer, the UK Prime Minister, is facing significant criticism from both opposition and some Labour MPs regarding the controversial appointment of Peter Mandelson as ambassador to the United Sta...

item.title: Evacuation Alert Issued in Draper, Alta., as Clearwater River Levels Rise
item.description: Residents of Draper, a small rural community of 137 people southeast of Fort McMurray, Alberta, have been placed on an evacuation alert as water levels rise along the Clearwater River due to melting i...

item.title: Mira Road stabbing: Guard recounts supervisor's distress during knife attack
item.description: In a shocking incident early Monday morning at an under-construction building near Wockhardt Hospital in Mira Road, Mumbai, a man named Zaib Zuber Ansari allegedly attacked security guards with a knif...

item.title: Damon Jones pleads guilty in US gambling investigation
item.description: Former NBA star Damon Jones has officially pleaded guilty as part of an ongoing investigation into gambling activities in the United States. The probe, which has attracted widespread attention, examin...

item.title: US imposes sanctions on two Indians and their firms for alleged involvement in global fentanyl supply chain
item.description: Two Indian nationals, Satishkumar Hareshbhai Sutaria and Yuktakumari Ashishkumar Modi, along with their companies, SR Chemicals and Agrat Chemicals, have been sanctioned by the United States for their...
